typography.instructions: Use Manrope for headings with tracking-tight. Use DM Sans for all body text, dashboard data, and standard UI components. Avoid Inter and space grotesk.

primary.description: Deep Tropical Green. Use sparingly for primary actions, active states, and brand moments.

background.description: Warm Sand / Off-white (stone-50) to keep interface airy and reduce eye strain compared to pure white.

surface.description: Pure white for cards and modules to create contrast against the warm sand background.

accent.description: Terracotta / Rust. Use for urgent CTAs, human handover queues, and alert badges.

secondary.description: Linen / Light Ochre for subtle active backgrounds and secondary badges.

text.description: Espresso/Charcoal for primary text to ensure 4.5:1 contrast. Sage/Olive for muted text.

visual_enhancers.borders: Use subtle 1px borders (border-stone-200) for structural separation. No pure black borders.
visual_enhancers.shadows: Ambient, soft shadows for elevated components (e.g., dropdowns). For standard dashboard cards, use a flat cardless approach with 1px border instead of heavy shadows.
visual_enhancers.radius: Max 8px (rounded-lg) to maintain a modern, slightly structural but approachable feel.
visual_enhancers.textures: Use slight grain overlays in hero areas or login pages if applicable, but keep dashboard clean for data legibility.

grids_and_layouts.dashboard: Use a High Density Grid (Bento style). Base grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4. Summary cards col-span-1. Charts col-span-2 or 3. Use tight gaps (gap-4 or gap-6).
grids_and_layouts.sidebar: Fixed left sidebar with earthy accent on active state. Must be collapsible for scaling.
grids_and_layouts.spacing: Generous spacing. p-6 or p-8 inside structural containers. Avoid cramped data tables.

components.cards: Go cardless or flat rectangular with 1px subtle border. No deep shadows for data widgets.
components.chat_simulator: Needs to look like a real messaging interface (WhatsApp style). Use light green (#DCF8C6 or similar tailwind equivalent like green-100) for user bubbles, white for AI bubbles, and a subtle textured background.
components.tables: Shadcn UI tables, left-aligned data, clear badges for status (Pending/Confirmed).
components.badges: Pill-shaped, using the secondary/accent palette (e.g., Terracotta for Cancelled/Urgent, Sage for Confirmed/Done).

motion.interactions: Micro-animations on hover (e.g., slight -translate-y-0.5 or border color change). Staggered entrance for list items.

accessibility.contrast: Text must meet 4.5:1 ratio. No transparent backgrounds on interactive overlays.
accessibility.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute in kebab-case.

image_urls.description: Tropical resort bedroom with wooden furniture
White bed linen with brown wooden bed frame
Variety of Indonesian food on table
Wooden table topped with bowls of food

instructions_to_main_agent: Use Phosphor Icons (@phosphor-icons/react) for a premium, duotone or light look across the dashboard.
Implement Shadcn UI components but heavily customize their default Tailwind classes to match the earthy, organic palette.
Ensure the Chat Simulator looks distinct from the rest of the dashboard, using a WhatsApp-inspired UI (light green bubbles, textured or solid light background).
Avoid using Shadcn's Card component excessively; opt for raw HTML div with 'border border-stone-200 rounded-lg bg-white'.
Do not use generic 'blue-purple' AI slop. Use the provided Deep Tropical Green and Terracotta accent colors.
In the Chat Simulator, ensure 'data-testid' is present on message bubbles, input fields, and the send button.
GENERAL_UI_UX_DESIGN_GUIDELINES: CRITICAL: DO NOT use complete transparent background in header, overlays, any surface that has content under it.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute to facilitate robust automated testing.
Avoid generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition (e.g. `transition: all`).
You MUST NOT center align the app container, i.e. do not add `.App { text-align: center; }`.
Always use modern button styles (pill-shaped or sharp) with interaction animations.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters like robot, brain, lightbulb, target, etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Use sonner for toasts, located in /app/src/components/ui/sonner.js.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects carefully, but DO NOT use transparent backgrounds on interactive dropdowns or overlays.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }`.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include `data-testid` attribute. Convention: kebab-case, role-based.
